name: United States (1908–1912)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 46 white five-pointed stars arranged in six rows of 8-7-8-8-7-8.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1908 following the admission of Oklahoma as the 46th state on 16 November 1907. The 46-star flag was used during the William Howard Taft administration and the high tide of the Progressive Era. It was replaced on 4 July 1912 after the admission of New Mexico (6 January 1912) and Arizona (14 February 1912) as the 47th and 48th states, completing the contiguous lower 48.

features.symbolism: the thirteen original British colonies that declared independence in 1776
the 46 states of the Union following the admission of Oklahoma
